chapter_title.en: Chapter on Currency Exchange and Its Forms

text: 4040 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : جَاءَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ سِجِسْتَانَ فَقَالَ إِنَّ عِنْدَنَا دَرَاهِمَ يُقَالُ لَهَا اَلشَّامِيَّةُ تَحْمِلُ عَلَى اَلدَّرَاهِمِ دَانِقَيْنِ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ يَجُوزُ ذَلِكَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4040 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that a man from Sijistan came to him and said:
"We have dirhams called al-Shamiyyah that are valued two daniqs more than regular dirhams."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it; it is permissible."
